oracle_data.fallback_response: I don't have more detail on that — check the codebase or the existing test cases for the exact behavior.

feature10_position_and_cursor.json.description: Exposes reader position tracking for parsed values and cursor-style seeking, position setting, and buffered reads over byte input.

cases.expected_output:
status=ok
a=65534
tracked_pos=2
initial_value=253
mutated_value=1
clone_value=1
clone_pos=2

initial_pos=0
start_seek=0
after_set=0
read0_len=4
read0_bytes=1,2,3,4
read0_pos=4
read1_len=1
read1_bytes=5,0,0,0
read1_pos=5

feature11_names_and_gates.json.description: Returns canonical byte-order names and evaluates zero-field record preconditions without reading payload bytes.

feature1_assertions.json.description: Parses a one-byte record that must satisfy a declared value condition, reporting success values or normalized assertion/custom failure details.

feature2_magic_and_padding.json.description: Validates literal marker bytes around binary records and applies declared byte padding before and after fields while exposing parsed field values or marker errors.

feature3_derived_field_controls.json.description: Reads records whose output fields are controlled by length prefixes, temporary values, caller-supplied booleans, defaults, and per-field byte order.

feature4_optional_and_pointer_reads.json.description: Parses fallible optional binary fields and pointer-based fields, preserving stream recovery semantics and resolving pointed-to values after parsing.

feature5_mapping_transforms.json.description: Transforms raw binary values through mapping and fallible conversion steps, returning transformed values or normalized conversion failures.

feature6_enum_selection.json.description: Selects binary enum variants using markers, numeric representations, endianness overrides, and preconditions, returning variant data or normalized no-match errors.

feature7_enum_error_modes.json.description: Controls how variant-selection failures are surfaced, either preserving per-variant failure details or collapsing unexpected failures into a single no-match category.

cases.expected_output:
error=variant_errors
pos=0
variant_error_count=2
variant=One
error=bad_magic
pos=0
variant=Two
error=unexpected_eof

feature8_string_parsing.json.description: Reads null-terminated byte strings and null-terminated wide strings, using the requested byte order for wide characters and returning decoded text values.

cases.expected_output:
status=ok
first=null terminated strings? in my system's language?
second=no thanks

feature9_punctuated_sequences.json.description: Reads counted sequences whose elements are separated by marker records, optionally requiring a trailing separator, and exposes item and separator counts.

cases.expected_output:
status=ok
count=3
len=3
values=[One, One, One]
separators=2

status=ok
count=3
len=3
values=[One, One, One]
separators=3
